notes: AI-facing tile legend for the scribble tileset (128×128 hand-drawn art over a 14-column × 11-row sheet, 154 tiles total). Generate a map as a Tiled tile layer: an object with "type": "tilelayer", a "name", integer "width" and "height", and a flat "data" array of length width*height. Each entry in "data" is the GID of a tile defined below (use the id shown as each tile's key, e.g. 8 for grass). GIDs assume the tileset is referenced at firstgid: 1 (the only configuration this codebase uses); GID = row*14 + col + 1 over the 14×11 tilesheet. The array is row-major: the first "width" entries are the top row, left to right, then the next row. Only use ids listed below; do not invent ids. TRANSPARENT TWINS: columns 8-13 (GIDs in the gaps 9-14, 23-28, 37-42, 51-56, 65-70, 79-84, 93-98, 107-112) are transparent-background copies of the tiles in columns 1-6 of the same row (shifted +7 columns; e.g. the tile at col 8 is the transparent version of the tile at col 1). Their names end in "_transparent". They are "layer": "object" overlays with no floor of their own — place them on top of a ground tile (e.g. a transparent wall over grass). Movement/sight blocking is copied from their floored twin. ROTATION / FLIP: most directional tiles (wall edges, corners, bridges, paths) ship only in one orientation; to render them at other angles, OR the GID with one of Tiled's standard flip flags: 0x80000000 (flip H), 0x40000000 (flip V), 0x20000000 (anti-diagonal flip, used for 90° rotations). Common combinations: rotate 90° CW = 0xA0000000 (+2684354560); rotate 180° = 0xC0000000 (+3221225472); rotate 270° CW = 0x60000000 (+1610612736). Maps may have two stacked tile layers: a "ground" layer (one of the "layer": "ground" tiles below per cell) and an optional "object" layer (one of the "layer": "object" tiles per cell, or 0 for empty) drawn on top. "blocksMovement" is authoritative for reachability/validation — a cell is impassable if either its ground tile or its object tile (if any) has blocksMovement true. "blocksSight" works the same way for line-of-sight: vision is blocked if either the ground or object tile has blocksSight true. Impassable terrain (chasm) can be crossed by placing a bridge or walkway tile over it (a passable object over an impassable ground opens the cell).

1.name: blank
1.description: Featureless light tile with no surface detail. Passable. Use sparingly — for default walkable floors prefer stone_floor (GID 15) or its variants.

8.name: grass
8.description: Solid medium-green grass. Default outdoor ground fill.

9.name: path_corner_se_transparent
9.description: Dirt path corner; the path enters from the bottom edge and turns to exit on the right. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

10.name: stone_wall_corner_tl_transparent
10.description: Solid stone wall corner piece (top-left). Blocks movement and sight. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

11.name: stone_wall_top_transparent
11.description: Solid stone wall segment placed along the top of the tile. Blocks movement and sight. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile. Twin of stone_wall_top.

12.name: doorway_top_transparent
12.description: An open doorway (no door) set in the top part of the tile. Passable opening; place over a wall tile. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile. Twin of doorway_top.

13.name: crate_small_transparent
13.description: A small wooden crate. Does not block sight or movement. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

14.name: wooden_plank_transparent
14.description: Light wooden plank overlay — a passable wooden surface drawn on top of an existing ground tile. Transparent background. Use to add wooden detail (boards, planking, decking) over a non-wood ground without replacing it.

15.name: stone_floor
15.description: Pale grey flagstone floor with faint grid lines. **Default walkable dungeon / interior floor** — pair with stone_floor_cracked (71) for variation in worn or ruined areas.

22.name: crate_closed
22.description: A closed crate with a slatted lid. Blocks movement and sight; searchable for loot.

23.name: path_straight_v_transparent
23.description: Straight vertical dirt path running from the bottom edge to the top edge. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

24.name: stone_wall_corner_curved_transparent
24.description: Curved stone wall corner piece. Blocks movement and sight. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

25.name: half_wall_ul_transparent
25.description: A low half-wall in the upper-left of the tile. Passable; provides cover but does not block line of sight. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

26.name: doorway_open_top_transparent
26.description: An open doorway with no door, set in the upper part of the tile. Passable opening. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

27.name: brazier_transparent
27.description: A standing lit brazier. Blocks movement; emits light. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

28.name: chair_n_transparent
28.description: A wooden chair, centered, facing north. Passable seat a character can occupy. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

36.name: trapdoor
36.description: A trapdoor set into the floor that opens toward the right. Passable; acts as a level transition.

37.name: path_intersection_transparent
37.description: A four-way dirt path intersection; paths meet in the center of the tile. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

38.name: stone_wall_corner_pit_transparent
38.description: Stone wall corner beside a void. Blocks movement and sight. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

39.name: half_wall_cracked_ul_transparent
39.description: A cracked low half-wall in the upper part of the tile. Passable; provides cover but does not block line of sight. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

40.name: door_open_top_transparent
40.description: An open door; frame in the upper part of the tile, door swung open toward the lower right. Passable. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

41.name: barrels_two_transparent
41.description: Two barrels in the center; one upright at top-right, one lying on its side at lower-left. Searchable. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

42.name: firewood_transparent
42.description: A pile of planks or firewood. Searchable. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

43.name: stone_floor_diamond
43.description: Stone floor with a diamond tile pattern. Walkable dungeon floor.

50.name: stairs_up
50.description: A staircase leading up. Passable; acts as a level transition.

51.name: rail_corner_se_transparent
51.description: Railway track curving from the bottom edge to the right edge. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

52.name: stone_wall_conduit_corner_transparent
52.description: Curved stone wall corner with a conduit/pipe. Blocks movement and sight. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

53.name: window_top_transparent
53.description: A horizontal window set into the top part of a wall. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

54.name: brazier_b_transparent
54.description: A standing lit brazier. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

55.name: barrels_three_transparent
55.description: Three barrels arranged along the vertical center of the tile. Searchable. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

56.name: bed_n_transparent
56.description: A bed, centered, vertical, facing north. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

57.name: stone_floor_inlay
57.description: Stone floor with a central diamond inlay. Walkable dungeon floor.

64.name: stairs_down
64.description: A staircase leading down. Passable; acts as a level transition.

65.name: rail_straight_v_transparent
65.description: Straight railway track running from the bottom edge to the top edge. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

66.name: stone_wall_corner_ul_transparent
66.description: Stone wall placed in the upper-left part of the tile. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

67.name: wall_spikes_top_transparent
67.description: A horizontal wall along the top of the tile with spikes facing toward the center. Deals contact damage. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

68.name: chest_s_transparent
68.description: A chest, facing south. Searchable. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

69.name: arrow_body_se_transparent
69.description: Marker: arrow body curving from the center-bottom toward the center-right. Part of a multi-tile directional arrow overlay. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

70.name: bed_unmade_transparent
70.description: An unmade bed. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

71.name: stone_floor_cracked
71.description: Cracked grey stone floor. Walkable dungeon floor.

78.name: bridge_v
78.description: A straight bridge segment running from top to bottom. Passable; lay across water or chasm to cross.

79.name: rail_intersection_transparent
79.description: A railway intersection; tracks meet in the center of the tile. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

80.name: stone_wall_corner_ul_b_transparent
80.description: Stone wall placed in the upper-left part of the tile. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

81.name: wall_broken_top_transparent
81.description: A broken-down wall along the top of the tile, rubble spilling toward the center. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

82.name: campfire_transparent
82.description: A campfire. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

83.name: arrow_body_v_transparent
83.description: Marker: vertical arrow body, centered, running from top to bottom. Part of a multi-tile directional arrow overlay. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

84.name: coffin_transparent
84.description: A coffin or tomb marked with a cross. Searchable. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

85.name: wood_floor
85.description: Warm wooden plank floor. Default walkable floor for taverns and wooden interiors.

92.name: bridge_broken_bottom
92.description: A bridge segment with a missing board at the bottom. Passable but broken at the south edge.

93.name: minecart_n_transparent
93.description: A minecart on a railway track, facing north. Blocks movement. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

94.name: stone_wall_curve_top_transparent
94.description: A horizontal stone wall in the upper part of the tile, curved on the right side. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

95.name: grate_square_transparent
95.description: A square floor grate. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

96.name: flowers_transparent
96.description: A patch of flowers. Low decoration; does not block movement. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

97.name: arrow_head_n_transparent
97.description: Marker: arrowhead pointing up (north). Endpoint of a multi-tile directional arrow overlay. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

98.name: liquid_floor_transparent
98.description: A pool of liquid on the ground. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

99.name: terrain_bumpy
99.description: Bumpy, uneven terrain. Walkable but counts as difficult terrain.

106.name: walkway_v
106.description: A straight elevated walkway running from top to bottom. Passable.

107.name: minecart_offrail_n_transparent
107.description: A minecart off the tracks, centered, facing north. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

108.name: stone_wall_corner_ul_top_transparent
108.description: A horizontal stone wall in the upper-left part of the tile. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

109.name: grate_round_transparent
109.description: A round floor grate. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

110.name: tree_transparent
110.description: A tree placed on top of a ground tile. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

111.name: arrow_start_n_transparent
111.description: Marker: start of a multi-tile arrow, beginning at center and continuing north. Part of a directional arrow overlay. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

112.name: dragon_skull_transparent
112.description: The skull of a dragon. Searchable. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile.

65534.name: void
65534.description: Pure black void / abyss. The renderer draws a solid black rectangle here (no sprite is sampled from the tileset) and the cell is impassable. Use this as the chasm fill — fill the impassable region with GID 65534 and lay bridge tiles (78 or 106) across it for crossings.
